title: Reduced Relative Clauses
description: Tighten relative clauses by dropping WHO/WHICH/THAT + BE and using participles directly.
summary_pt_br: Reduzir oração relativa: 'The woman sitting there' = 'The woman who is sitting'. Só quando relative pronoun é sujeito.

scenes.subtitle_en: Tighten relative clauses by dropping WHO/WHICH/THAT + BE and using participles directly.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Reduzir oração relativa: 'The woman sitting there' = 'The woman who is sitting'. Só quando relative pronoun é sujeito.

scenes.text_en: Tighten relative clauses by dropping WHO/WHICH/THAT + BE and using participles directly. Today's focus: reduced relative clauses. The man standing by the door is my brother.
scenes.text_pt: Reduzir oração relativa: 'The woman sitting there' = 'The woman who is sitting'. Só quando relative pronoun é sujeito. Foco de hoje: reduced relative clauses. O homem em pé na porta é meu irmão.

scenes.title: Reduced Relative Clauses
scenes.body_en: 'The woman WHO IS sitting there' → 'The woman SITTING there'. 'The package WHICH WAS delivered' → 'The package DELIVERED'. Only works when the relative pronoun is subject.
scenes.body_pt: Reduzir oração relativa: 'The woman sitting there' = 'The woman who is sitting'. Só quando relative pronoun é sujeito.

examples.en: The man standing by the door is my brother.
examples.pt: O homem em pé na porta é meu irmão.

examples.en: The laptops shipped last week arrived today.
examples.pt: Os notebooks enviados semana passada chegaram hoje.

examples.en: Anyone interested should send an email.
examples.pt: Qualquer um interessado deve mandar email.

exercise.question: Complete: "The man ___ by the door is my brother."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The man standing by the door is my brother."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O homem em pé na porta é meu irmão.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The laptops shipped last week arrived today.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The laptops shipped last week arrived today."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Os notebooks enviados semana passada chegaram hoje.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Reduced Relative Clauses — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre reduced relative clauses. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Try reducing: 'The students who were chosen will travel.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now: 'The man who is sitting over there.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Reduce: 'Anyone who is interested in the trip.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Try passive. 'The reports which were written by Ana.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Can you reduce both active and passive together?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The book written by her and loved by fans.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Clean, tight, and still correct. Well done.

scenes.title: Editing for concision
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize three reductions Zara demonstrated. Use at least two reduced relative clauses.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In Saturday's advanced class, Zara opened a stack of essays and began cutting extra words.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Any clause starting with 'who is' or 'which was' can almost always be shortened.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Does reducing always sound more formal, or only in some contexts?

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Formal and journalistic writing love reductions, but casual speech tends to keep full clauses.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So the passengers traveling with pets should check in earlier becomes the shortened version?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Editing, Zara said, is the only real proof that a writer respects the reader's time.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Reduced Relative Clauses". Tighten relative clauses by dropping WHO/WHICH/THAT + BE and using participles directly. The man standing by the door is my brother. The laptops shipped last week arrived today. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Reduced Relative Clauses". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Reduced Relative Clauses".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
